mission.title: Install and Configure Trickster on Kubernetes
mission.description: Trickster is an open-source HTTP reverse proxy/cache designed to accelerate time series dashboard queries and improve the performance of HTTP applications. Installing Trickster allows you to enhance your application's response times and caching capabilities.

steps.title: Add the Trickster Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Trickster Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add trickster https://charts.appscode.com/stable
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Trickster using Helm
steps.description:
Install Trickster in a new namespace called 'trickster' with the following command:
```bash
helm install trickster trickster/trickster --namespace trickster --create-namespace --version v2.0.1
```

steps.title: Verify Trickster installation
steps.description:
Check if the Trickster pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace trickster
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Trickster deployment to set resource limits. Create a file named `trickster-limits.yaml` with the following content:
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: trickster
  namespace: trickster
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: trickster
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
``` 
Then apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f trickster-limits.yaml
```

steps.title: Port forward Trickster service
steps.description:
To access Trickster locally, set up port forwarding:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/trickster --namespace trickster 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Trickster will show the Trickster pods running in the 'trickster' namespace, and you will be able to access it via port 8080 on your local machine. Resource limits will be configured as specified.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add trickster https://charts.appscode.com/stable
helm repo update
helm install trickster trickster/trickster --namespace trickster --create-namespace --version v2.0.1

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: trickster
  namespace: trickster
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: trickster
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Trickster release from the Kubernetes cluster using Helm with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall trickster --namespace trickster
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Trickster. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace trickster
kubectl delete crd tricksters.trickstercache.org
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the Trickster namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace trickster
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current Trickster configuration and data. You can use the following command to export the current deployment:
```bash
kubectl get deployment trickster --namespace trickster -o yaml > trickster-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Trickster Helm repository and upgrade Trickster to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade trickster trickster/trickster --namespace trickster --version v2.0.2
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Trickster pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace trickster
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If Trickster pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace trickster
``` 
Look for any configuration issues or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Trickster service, ensure that the service is running and the port forwarding is set up correctly:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace trickster
kubectl port-forward service/trickster --namespace trickster 8080:80
```

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
If Trickster is consuming too much memory, check the resource limits and adjust them if necessary. You can view the resource usage with:
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace trickster
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration changes not applied
troubleshooting.description:
If changes to the Trickster configuration are not reflected, ensure that the deployment was updated correctly. Reapply the configuration file:
```bash
kubectl apply -f trickster-limits.yaml --namespace trickster
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed on your local machine.
